ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
Off Calendar Oct-31-2022 Continued To Dec-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tabia Harris, via Zoom, with son Corey Davis, in propria persona, Personal Representative.

The court notes the numerous continuances of this matter and acknowledges the declaration filed with the court wherein there is yet an additional request for a continuance. Mr. Davis provides the court with an update on behalf of his mother and advises that the paralegal who is assisting his mother with this matter advised that the Examiner's notes can likely be cleared by the middle of December. After additional discussion, the court advises that it intends to grant the request for a continuance with the caveat that all of the Examiner's notes must be cleared before the next hearing date.

The subject matter is continued to Dec-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tabia Harris Davis, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court asks Ms. Harris regarding the status of a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval on Status of Administration. The court and Ms. Davis discuss the status of the Estate with Ms. Harris advising that creditors have been notified. Ms. Harris' son advises that he will assist his mother in preparing the Petition. The court advises that it will be continuing the matter and instructs Ms. Harris to notify the assigned Examiner before the next calling of the case if she requires additional time.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Dec-07-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tabia Davis appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Ms. Davis what motivated her to file for probate and presented a series of questions with regard to her understanding of administration of an estate. Ms. Davis stated that her Brother had a lawsuit and she wanted to make sure his assets were in the family's possession, that the case settled and that the proceeds are with the Lawyer in the attorney client trust account. Ms. Davis also stated that she has been trying to get the proceeds for several years, that she gave the Lawyer the document for the small estate, but the Lawyer didn't go for it, that there are no other siblings, that there are no pre-deceased siblings and that they were the only two in the family. In addition, Ms. Davis stated that she is not aware of any other assets, that she is pretty confident that there are no other assets, that she never administered an estate, that she looked things up on line and read everything, and that her Son helped her. Furthermore, Ms. Davis stated that she took care of her Mother and had to deal with things for her Mother, that she knows and understands what she can and can't do, that she knows she has to put the money in an estate account and that she has a money market account. The Court informed Ms. Davis that a money market account is considered an investment account, that it should be an interest-bearing account, and that she can't co-mingle her own funds with estate funds. Ms. Davis stated that she understands that she can't the funds without a Court order, that if the petition is granted, she will place the funds in an account and that she will wait to see if her Brother had any liabilities. Ms. Davis also stated that she understands that she has to keep records and the requirement to provide notice to the Dept. of Health Care Services, that she has a letter from Medi-Cal showing that her Brother does not owe them anything, The Court informed Ms. Davis of the requirement to provide courtesy copies to the Court. Ms. Davis stated that she feels competent that she can comply with the requirement to bring the estate to a close in a reasonable amount of time. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition. Bond is not required. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for January 26, 2021 and status on close of estate is set for September 28, 2021 (both) at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Davis that if the docuemtns are not filed one week before the hearing dates, she will have to come in and explain why they haven't been filed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
